task: VOID-EVERYWHERE PR-2 (gated, default OFF, NO migration). Extends the PR-1 shared void engine to Bills -- the AP mirror of the invoice (AR) wiring. void.service.ts: VoidableEntityType gains 'bill'; readOriginalGlPostings reads the bill's posted GL lines by source_transaction_type='bill' (the posting-engine source type from buildBillLines); auditVoid maps bill -> accounting.bills. bills.service.ts voidBill becomes flag-aware (same shape as the invoice route): flag OFF = unchanged legacy behaviour (Owner-only, status flip to 'void' + revoked_at/revoked_by/revoked_reason + crud audit, no reversing entry); flag ON = enforce VOID=Owner+Accountant + required reason, post the equal-and-opposite reversing JE on the SAME transaction client (closed-period routed to the current open period via accounting.closed_period_cutoff), then mark VOIDED + audit spine. Adds an idempotency guard (a bill already voided cannot be voided again). bills.routes.ts /void drops its hard Owner-only pre-check (role now enforced in the flag-aware service) and maps the new errors (403 forbidden_*; 400 void_reason_required; 409 bill_already_void). No schema change: bills already carry status + revoked_at/revoked_by_user_id/revoked_reason + bill_date; reversal linkage lives in the audit spine + the reversing JE, exactly like invoices. Unit test adds the bill-shaped reversal case (DR Expense/CR AP -> net zero, class+vendor preserved). Build/test on TRANSP. GUARD verifies the bill reversing-entry diff vs QuickBooks before merge; never self-merge. PR-3 extends the same engine to Expenses; PR-4 to Settlements.
